content: Hi, I'm looking for some social events in my area. I'm Ezra, 57, and I spend a lot of my weekends volunteering at the Paws & Claws animal shelter, so I'm usually free during the week evenings.

Hello Ezra! It's wonderful that you dedicate your weekends to volunteering at Paws & Claws. To help me find suitable events, could you tell me a bit about your interests? What kind of activities do you enjoy?
Well, I'm an indie game developer, but outside of work, I'm quite active. I love hiking, woodworking, and mountain biking. I also dabble in painting and photography, especially drone photography.
That's a fantastic range of interests, Ezra! Let's see... how about a photography meet-up or a nature-focused event? Given your love for hiking and photography, that seems like a good fit.

A photography meet-up sounds great! Especially if it involves drone photography. I'm always looking to share tips or learn new techniques. Is there anything local this week or next? I'm free Tuesday and Thursday evenings.

I've found a 'Drone Photography Enthusiasts' group that meets every second Thursday at the community center. Their next meeting is this coming Thursday, April 4th, at 7 PM. They often do joint outdoor shoots on weekends, too.
April 4th, 7 PM, at the community center. That's perfect! I'll mark that down. I've been wanting to connect with other drone pilots. Thanks for finding that.
You're very welcome, Ezra! Would you like me to look for any other events, perhaps a woodworking workshop or a guided hike that aligns with your free weekday evenings?
A guided hike might be pushing it for a weekday evening, but if there's a woodworking class that isn't too late, I'd be interested. My garage workshop is getting a bit dusty lately.
I understand. I'm searching for woodworking classes now. There's a 'Basic Wood Carving' class starting next Monday, April 8th, at the local craft studio. It runs from 6 PM to 8 PM for four weeks.
Hmm, four weeks might be a bit much right now. I'll stick with the drone meet-up for April 4th. But thanks for the option!
No problem at all, Ezra. I'm glad I could help you find something you're excited about. Is there anything else I can assist you with today?
That's all for now. Thanks a bunch!
